SEPARATE STATEMENT OF FACTS IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFF TEMUR JUMANOV?S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE (TRANSACTION ID # 78906547) FILED BY DEFENDANT VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.

DISCOVERY 301, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE; DEMAND FOR SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 22, 2026 TO GIVE THE PARTIES A FULL OPPORTUNITY TO ENGAGE IN MEANINGFUL MEET AND CONFER EFFORTS. NO LATER THAN THE END OF THE DAY ON APRIL 17, 2026 THE PARTIES SHALL MEET IN PERSON OR BY REMOTE VIDEO PLATFORM TO MEANINGFULLY DISCUSS EACH UNRESOLVED ISSUE RAISED BY THIS MOTION. NO LATER THAN 4PM ON APRIL 20, 2026 COUNSEL FOR JUMANOV SHALL SEND AN EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT301TR@SFTC.ORG WITH A COPY TO COUNSEL FOR VOLKSWAGEN IDENTIFYING, WITHOUT ARGUMENT, ANY REMAINING UNRESOLVED ISSUES THAT NEED TO BE DECIDED BY THE COURT. FOR GUIDANCE OF THE PARTIES, ALL DOCUMENTS RELATING TO JUMANOV'S VEHICLE ARE PRESUMPTIVELY DISCOVERABLE AND ALL DOCUMENTS RELATING TO OTHER VEHICLES OF THE SAME MAKE AND MODEL ARE PRESUMPTIVELY DISCOVERABLE ONLY IF THEY ARE READILY AVAILABLE TO VOLKSWAGEN WITHOUT NEED TO REVIEW OR ANALYZE DOCUMENTS IN A SPECIFIC CAR OWNER'S FILES OR THE REPAIR HISTORY OF A SPECIFIC VEHICLE. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-22-26 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 301

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, APR-22-2026 HEARING HELD RE: PLAINTIFF TEMUR JUMANOV'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE; DEMAND FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NOS. 6, 7, AND 18 (THE JUMANOV CAR SPECIFIC REQUESTS), AND REQUESTS NOS. 3, 4, AND 34 FOR CARS WITH THE SAME MAKE, MODEL, YEAR, AND ALLEGED DEFECTS. ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS AS TO THESE REQUESTS ARE TO BE PRODUCED WITHIN 45 DAYS FROM TODAY'S DATE AND ARE TO BE SUBJECT TO A PROTECTIVE ORDER USING THE STANDARD LOS ANGELES SUPERIOR COURT PROTECTIVE ORDER FOR LEMON LAW CASES. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: S. KANE, NOT REPORTED (301/HEK)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; Demand For Sanctions; And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-22-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ERIK J. KHOROVSKY, Esq. of Seven Law Group, APC (Tel: 310-525-2244) for plaintiff Temur Jumanov;
Atty. SOPHIE PAENG, Esq. of Squire Patton Boggs (Tel: 213-624-2500) for defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF TEMUR JUMANOV's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; Demand For Sanctions.

The court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear.

Matter argued and submitted.

The court rules as follows: Plaintiff's motion is granted as to requests for production of documents nos. 6, 7, and 18 (the Jumanov car specific requests), and requests nos. 3, 4, and 34 for cars with the same make, model, year, and alleged defects. All responsive documents as to these requests are to be produced within 45 days from today's date and are to be subject to a protective order using the standard Los Angeles Superior Court protective order for Lemon Law cases.

The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: S. Kane, Not Reported (301/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; Demand For Sanctions; And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-13-2026 Continued To Apr-22-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE; DEMAND FOR SANCTIONS

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Continued to April 22, 2026 to give the parties a full opportunity to engage in meaningful meet and confer efforts. No later than the end of the day on April 17, 2026 the parties shall meet in person or by remote video platform to meaningfully discuss each unresolved issue raised by this motion. No later than 4pm on April 20, 2026 counsel for Jumanov shall send an email to Contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to counsel for Volkswagen identifying, without argument, any remaining unresolved issues that need to be decided by the court. For guidance of the parties, all documents relating to Jumanov's vehicle are presumptively discoverable and all documents relating to other vehicles of the same make and model are presumptively discoverable only if they are readily available to Volkswagen without need to review or analyze documents in a specific car owner's files or the repair history of a specific vehicle. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)
